text.title: Client Firing and Graceful Offboarding Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of `Client Firing and Graceful Offboarding` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: The decision MUST cite ≥ 3 documented friction events from the last 90 days (date, summary, evidence link). 'Vibes' or single events forbidden as firing rationale.
rule.rationale: Vibes-based firings cause founders to second-guess for months; the 3-event threshold both protects the client (no rash decisions) and the founder (clean rationale on file).

rule.statement: Notice period MUST match the contract clause (or 30 days minimum if silent). Same-day terminations are forbidden except for safety / harassment.
rule.rationale: Same-day terminations escalate to legal disputes; honouring notice keeps the exit clean.

rule.statement: Offboarding MUST ship a handoff document (creds, runbooks, asset inventory, ongoing-task status) signed by founder and client before the relationship ends.
rule.rationale: Un-documented handoffs cause 30-60 days of follow-up emails after termination; the signed handoff doc kills the long-tail.

rule.statement: Where applicable, the offboarding artefact MUST name at least one referral alternative the client can engage. 'You'll figure it out' is forbidden.
rule.rationale: Naming an alternative converts most clients from adversarial to grateful; their next call is to the alternative, not to small-claims.

rule.statement: Within 14 days of offboarding completion, founder MUST record a post-mortem (what to avoid in intake, what to clarify in contract, ICP refinement). Postmortem-skipped means the next bad client comes through the same door.
rule.rationale: Skipped post-mortems mean repeated bad-fit intakes; the 14-day window forces founder to extract the lesson while fresh.

rule.statement: When preconditions in `Applies If` fail OR any item in `Skip If` triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
